nodes.label: AI as research efficiency tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to personal learning enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: AI is essentially doing that initial Google search and general reading and compiling it for me

nodes.label: AI accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: it's still prone to inaccuracy and hallucination

nodes.label: AI as idea generator and suggestion provider
nodes.label_negative: AI as source of incorrect details
nodes.grounding_span: It provided me with a list of suggestions and, most importantly, reasons for those suggestions

nodes.label: Personal learning and enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: I enjoy learning about things for myself, so I don't want to give up that part of the process

nodes.label: Efficiency and convenience
nodes.grounding_span: makes the process a little more efficient and convenient

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI for research efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: it's sometimes convenient for rounding up general information and giving me a starting point

nodes.label: Skepticism toward AI due to inaccuracy
nodes.grounding_span: it's still prone to inaccuracy and hallucination

nodes.label: Resistance to over-reliance on AI for learning
nodes.grounding_span: I don't necessarily want my information summarized and spoon fed to me

nodes.label: Self-directed learning preference — enjoys independent discovery
nodes.grounding_span: I enjoy learning about things for myself, so I don't want to give up that part of the process

nodes.label: Analogical reasoning — compares AI to past technological shifts
nodes.grounding_span: In much the same way that pre-computer, I had to go to the library...now using AI makes the process a little more efficient
